solution: 
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <cmath>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> constructRectangle(int area) {
        int max_w = 1;
        int sqrt_area = static_cast<int>(sqrt(area));
        for (int w = 1; w <= sqrt_area; ++w) {
            if (area % w == 0) {
                max_w = w;
            }
        }
        return {area / max_w, max_w};
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(√area)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently finds the optimal dimensions by iterating up to the square root of the area, ensuring the smallest possible difference between length and width while satisfying the constraints.
